##NAMES=Osumilite-(Mg)
##RRUFFID=R141102
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=KMg_2_Al_3_(Al_2_Si_10_)O_30_
##LOCALITY=Caspar quarry, Bellerberg volcano, Mayen, Eifel, Germany
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Ch. and H. Schäfer
##DESCRIPTION=Pale blue short prismatic or tabular hexagonal crystals, associated with acicular mullite, colorless sanidine, reddish rutile.
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed only by single crystal X-ray diffraction
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R141102
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 10.076 b: 10.076 c: 14.331 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 120 volume: 1260 crystal system: hexagonal
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
